text.title: Shadow Traffic Rollout Pattern Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns from real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: 1% real-traffic split; CSAT regression hits real users before joint-metric data.
antipattern.root-cause: Skipped shadow stage; jumped straight to flag-gated real traffic.
antipattern.fix: Mandatory 100% shadow window first; flag rollout only after gate passes.

antipattern.symptom: Candidate +0.05 quality but +200% cost. Promoted. Bill explodes the next month.
antipattern.fix: Add latency + cost dimensions to gate; ALL THREE must be within contract.

antipattern.symptom: Judge upgraded mid-window. Candidate scores higher because of the new judge, not because the candidate is better.
antipattern.root-cause: Judge model not pinned for the shadow window duration.
antipattern.fix: Pin judge version + prompt + calibration date at shadow start; any change voids the window and restarts.

antipattern.symptom: 6-hour window during work hours promotes candidate that fails on weekend consumer traffic.
antipattern.root-cause: Window <48h or n<500 or no weekend coverage.
antipattern.fix: Hard gate: refuse evaluation with window<48h OR n<500 OR coverage gaps.
